AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED ALONG AN INTERMITTENT 4 MILE PATH FROM NORTHEASTERN BARREN COUNTY INTO FAR NORTHERN METCALFE COUNTY. IT FIRST TOUCHED DOWN AS AN EF-O TORNADO JUST NORTHEAST OF HISEVILLE. THEN IT DID EF-1 DAMAGE ALONG ITS PATH BEFORE LIFTING ABOUT ONE MILE SOUTH OF THE TOWN OF CENTER. A HOME WAS DAMAGED AS WELL AS SEVERAL OUT-BUILDINGS AND BARNS...AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE TORNADO WAS 30 YARDS WIDE AND ENDED 7 MINUTES LATER AT 411 PM EDT.

AN EF 1 TORNADO WAS FOUND ALONG AN 18 MILE INTERMITTENT PATH FROM THE WARREN EDMONSON COUNTY LINE INTO NORTHWEST BARREN COUNTY. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN IN WARREN COUNTY 6.5 MILES SW OF BROWNSVILLE AND EVENTUALLY LIFTED IN BARREN COUNTY AROUND 727 PM CDT 6 MILES WEST OF GLASGOW. MAXIMUM WINDS AND WIDTH WERE 105 MPH AND 150 YARDS RESPECTIVELY. DAMAGE TO SEVERAL OUT BUILDINGS...HOMES AND TREES WERE FOUND.

AN EF 1 TORNADO WAS FOUND ALONG AN INTERMITTENT AND SHORT 1.3 MILE TRACK BEGINNING 1.2 MILES WNW OF THE TOWN OF CYCLONE IN SOUTHERN METCALFE COUNTY UNTIL IT LIFTED 0.3 MILES SOUTH OF CYCLONE IN NORTHERN MONROE COUNTY. THE TORNADO ONLY LASTED ABOUT 3 MINUTES...BUT WAS ABLE TO BECOME 150 YARDS WIDE AT ONE POINT AND PRODUCED 105 MPH WINDS. MINOR DAMAGE TO A ROOF AND PORCH OF TWO SEPARATE HOUSES WAS FOUND...ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO TREES AND A COUPLE OF BARNS.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO THAT TOUCHED DOWN 8.3 MILES NORTH OF SCOTTSVILLE NEAR US 235 AND LIFTED NORTH OF KY 1533. TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1 MILE AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS.
